member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex
member.summary: Represents a boot option index, which is a 16-bit unsigned integer value used to identify a specific boot option in the system's firmware. The structure provides an implicit conversion to and from ushort, as well as a string representation in the format "BootXXXX", where XXXX is the hexadecimal representation of the index value.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.#ctor(System.UInt16)
member.summary: Represents a boot option index, which is a 16-bit unsigned integer value used to identify a specific boot option in the system's firmware. The structure provides an implicit conversion to and from ushort, as well as a string representation in the format "BootXXXX", where XXXX is the hexadecimal representation of the index value.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.op_Equality(Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex,Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Determines whether two BootOptionIndex instances are equal.
member.param: The first BootOptionIndex to compare.
The second BootOptionIndex to compare.
member.returns: true if the specified BootOptionIndex instances are equal; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.op_Inequality(Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex,Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)
member.summary: Determines whether two BootOptionIndex instances are not equal.
member.param: The first BootOptionIndex to compare.
The second BootOptionIndex to compare.
member.returns: true if the specified BootOptionIndex instances are not equal; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.ToString
member.summary: Returns a string that represents boot option index in format "BootXXXX", where XXXX is the hexadecimal representation of the index value.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.op_Implicit(Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)~System.UInt16
member.summary: Impictly converts structure to

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.op_Implicit(System.UInt16)~Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex
member.summary: Implicitly converts value to structure.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex.op_Implicit(Unified.Firmware.BootOptionIndex)~System.String
member.summary: Converts a BootOptionIndex instance to its string representation.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment
member.summary: GLobally defined UEFI environment variables. Documentation : https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/03_Boot_Manager.html#globally-defined-variables

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.#ctor(Unified.Firmware.IFirmwareBackend)
member.summary: GLobally defined UEFI environment variables. Documentation : https://uefi.org/specs/UEFI/2.10/03_Boot_Manager.html#globally-defined-variables

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.AuditMode
member.summary: Whether the system is operating in Audit Mode (1) or not (0). All other values are reserved. Should be treated as read-only except when DeployedMode is 0. Always becomes read-only after ExitBootServices() is called. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.BootCurrent
member.summary: The boot option that was selected for the current boot. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.BootNext
member.summary: The boot option for the next boot only. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.BootOrder
member.summary: The ordered boot option load list. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.BootOptionSupport
member.summary: The types of boot options supported by the boot manager. Should be treated as read-only. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.DeployedMode
member.summary: Whether the system is operating in Deployed Mode (1) or not (0). All other values are reserved. Should be treated as read-only when its value is 1. Always becomes read-only after ExitBootServices() is called. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.DevAuthBoot
member.summary: Whether the platform firmware is operating in device authentication boot mode (1) or not (0). All other values are reserved. Should be treated as read-only. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.DriverOrder
member.summary: The ordered driver load option list. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.HwErrRecSupport
member.summary: Identifies the level of hardware error record persistence support implemented by the platform. This variable is only modified by firmware and is read-only to the OS. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.Lang
member.summary: The language code that the system is configured for. This value is deprecated. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.LangCodes
member.summary: The language codes that the firmware supports. This value is deprecated. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.OsIndications
member.summary: Allows the OS to request the firmware to enable certain features and to take certain actions. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.OsIndicationsSupported
member.summary: Allows the firmware to indicate supported features and actions to the OS. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.OsRecoveryOrder
member.summary: OS-specified recovery options. Variable attributes : BS, RT, NV, AT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.PlatformLangCodes
member.summary: The language codes that the firmware supports. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.PlatformLang
member.summary: The language code that the system is configured for. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.SignatureSupport
member.summary: Array of GUIDs representing the type of signatures supported by the platform firmware. Should be treated as read-only. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.SecureBoot
member.summary: Whether the platform firmware is operating in Secure boot mode (1) or not (0). All other values are reserved. Should be treated as read-only. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.SetupMode
member.summary: Whether the system should require authentication on SetVariable() requests to Secure Boot policy variables (0) or not (1). Should be treated as read-only. The system is in "Setup Mode" when SetupMode==1, AuditMode==0, and DeployedMode==0. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.Timeout
member.summary: The firmware's boot managers timeout, in seconds, before initiating the default boot selection. Variable attributes : NV, BS, RT

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.EnvironmentVendor.GlobalFirmwareEnvironment.VendorKeys
member.summary: Whether the system is configured to use only vendor-provided keys or not. Should be treated as read-only. Variable attributes : BS, RT

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.ReadStringVariable(System.String,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes@)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of a environment variable as a string.
member.remarks: The returned value is read from the environment. If the specified variable is not found, the method returns null. This method allocates and frees unmanaged memory internally, callers do not need to manage memory for the returned string.
member.param: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: A string containing the value of the specified environment variable, or null if the variable does not exist.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.WriteStringVariable(System.String,System.String,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes)
member.summary: Sets a environment variable with the specified name to the provided string value.
member.remarks: The variable is set globally and may affect other processes or components that read environment variables. The value is stored as a Unicode string. Ensure that the variable name and value conform to any platform-specific restrictions.
member.param: The name of the environment variable to set. Cannot be null or empty.
The string value to assign to the environment variable. Cannot be null.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.ReadVariable``1(System.String,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes@)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of a environment variable and returns it as a value type.
member.remarks: This method reads the variable from the environment and marshals it to the specified value type. The caller must ensure that the variable exists and is compatible with the requested type. If the variable does not exist or cannot be marshaled to type T, the result may be undefined.
member.typeparam: The value type to which the environment variable will be marshaled. Must be a struct.
member.param: The name of the environment variable to read. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: The value of the specified environment variable, marshaled to type T.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.WriteVariable``1(System.String,``0,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes)
member.summary: Writes a value of a specified struct type to a environment variable identified by name.
member.remarks: This method stores the value as a binary representation using unmanaged memory. The caller should ensure that the variable name is unique and valid within the environment. Only value types (structs) are supported.
member.typeparam: The struct type of the value to write to the environment variable.
member.param: The name of the environment variable to set. Cannot be null or empty.
The value to assign to the environment variable.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.ReadArrayVariable``1(System.String,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes@)
member.summary: Retrieves the value of a environment variable as an array of value type elements.
member.remarks: The method reads the raw memory of the environment variable and interprets it as an array of the specified value type. Use this method only when the variable is known to contain binary data compatible with the type T. The caller is responsible for ensuring the variable's format matches the expected type.
member.typeparam: The value type of the elements to read from the environment variable.
member.param: The name of the environment variable to retrieve. Cannot be null or empty.
member.returns: An array of elements of type T containing the values stored in the specified environment variable. The array will be empty if the variable contains no data.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.WriteArrayVariable``1(System.String,``0[],Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes)
member.summary: Writes a environment variable as an array of value type elements.
member.remarks: This method pins the array in memory and writes its contents to the specified environment variable. The variable is set as a contiguous block of memory representing the array. Use caution when passing large arrays, as this operation involves unmanaged memory handling.
member.typeparam: The value type of the elements in the array to be written.
member.param: The name of the environment variable to set. Cannot be null or empty.
The array of value type elements to write. Cannot be null and must contain at least one element.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironment.PinnedMemoryToArray``1(System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts a pointer to pinned memory into a managed array of type T.
member.param: The pointer to the memory.
The number of elements to read.
member.returns: An array containing the data from the memory.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironmentException
member.summary: Represents an error that occurred while working with UEFI

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareEnvironmentException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Represents an error that occurred while working with UEFI

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareInterface.Available
member.summary: Checks whether the UEFI platform is available on this system

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareInterface.SystemPartition
member.summary: Searches among the partition for the one that is marked as EfiSystemPartition

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.FirmwareInterface.BootToUserInterface
member.summary: Boot into the UEFI user interface after rebooting the computer. Does NOT reboot the computer, but sets the boot condition

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.IFirmwareBackend.CheckFirmwareAvailablity
member.summary: Checks whether the UEFI platform is available on this system

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.IFirmwareBackend.FindEfiSystemPartition
member.summary: Seraches fro GUID identifier of the system EFI partition. This is a unique identifier assigned to the partition when it is created, and can be used to identify the partition among other partitions on the same disk. It is not related to the partition type, and is not a constant value. It can be used to access the partition's files and directories or passed to Win32 API functions that require a volume path.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.IFirmwareBackend.ReadEnvironmentVariable(System.String,System.Guid,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes@,System.Int32,System.UInt32@)
member.summary: Gets the value of an environment variable with the specified name and attributes from the specified environment.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.IFirmwareBackend.WriteEnvironmentVariable(System.String,System.Guid,Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes,System.IntPtr,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the value of an environment variable with the specified name and attributes in the specified environment. The variable is stored in non-volatile memory and will persist across system reboots. The variable is associated with the specified environment, which is identified by a GUID. The attributes parameter specifies the attributes of the variable, such as whether it is read-only or can be accessed from user mode. The value parameter is a pointer to a buffer that contains the data to be stored in the variable, and ptrSize specifies the size of the buffer in bytes.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications
member.summary: The firmware and an Operating System may exchange information through the OsIndicationsSupported and the OSIndications variables as follows

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.None
member.summary: Nothing

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.BOOT_TO_FW_UI
member.summary: Once the firmware consumes this bit in the OsIndications variable and stops at the firmware user interface

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.TIMESTAMP_REVOCATION
member.summary: If the firmware supports timestamp based revocation and the � dbt � uthorized timestamp database variable.

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.FILE_CAPSULE_DELIVERY_SUPPORTED
member.summary: When submitting capsule via the Mass Storage Device method of Delivery of Capsules via file on Mass Storage Device, the bit EFI_OS_INDICATIONS_FILE_CAPSULE_DELIVERY_SUPPORTED in OsIndications variable must be set by submitter to trigger processing of submitted capsule on next reboot

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.FMP_CAPSULE_SUPPORTED
member.summary: If platform supports processing of Firmware Management Protocol update capsule as defined in Dependency Expression Instruction Set

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.CAPSULE_RESULT_VAR_SUPPORTED
member.summary: If platform supports reporting of deferred capsule processing by creation of result variable as defined in UEFI variable reporting on the Success or any Errors encountered in processing of capsules after restart

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.START_OS_RECOVERY
member.summary: Indicate that OS-defined recovery should commence upon reboot

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.START_PLATFORM_RECOVERY
member.summary: Indicate that Platform-defined recovery should commence upon reboot

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.OsIndications.JSON_CONFIG_DATA_REFRESH
member.summary: Bit is set in the OsIndications variable by submitter to trigger collecting current configuration and reporting the refreshed data to EFI System Configuration Table on next boot

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.IoctlVolumeEnumerable
member.summary: Enumerable collection of volumes by physical hard drive index

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.IoctlVolumeEnumerable.GetEnumerator
member.summary: Create new instance of 's enumerator

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.IoctlVolumeEnumerable.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create new instance of

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.IoctlVolumeInformation
member.summary: Provides methods for retrieving volume information using IOCTL commands.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.IoctlVolumeInformation.GetPartition(Unified.Firmware.VolumePath)
member.summary: Retrieves extended partition information for the specified volume.
member.param: The path to the volume.
member.returns: A structure containing the partition information.
member.exception: Thrown when the partition descriptor cannot be opened or the information cannot be retrieved.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.EfiPartitionAttribute
member.summary: The Extensible Firmware Interface (EFI) attributes of the partition.

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.EfiPartitionAttribute.PlatformRequired
member.summary: If this attribute is set, the partition is required by a computer to function properly.

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.EfiPartitionAttribute.NoDriveLetter
member.summary: If this attribute is set, the partition does not receive a drive letter by default when the disk is moved to another computer or when the disk is seen for the first time by a computer.

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.EfiPartitionAttribute.Hidden
member.summary: If this attribute is set, the partition is not detected by the Mount Manager.

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.EfiPartitionAttribute.ShadowCopy
member.summary: If this attribute is set, the partition is a shadow copy of another partition.

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.EfiPartitionAttribute.ReadOnly
member.summary: If this attribute is set, the partition is read-only.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.PartitionStyle
member.summary: Specifies the partition style used on a disk, such as Master Boot Record (MBR), GUID Partition Table (GPT), or raw (uninitialized).

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.DRIVE_LAYOUT_INFORMATION_EX
member.summary: Contains extended information about a drive's partitions.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.PARTITION_INFORMATION_GPT
member.summary: Contains GUID partition table (GPT) partition information.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.PARTITION_INFORMATION_EX
member.summary: Contains information about a disk partition.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.PlatformBackend.Win32Platform.SystemEnvironmentPrivilege
member.summary: Manages the temporary elevation of the current thread to enable the system environment privilege.
member.remarks: This class acquires the system environment privilege for the lifetime of the instance on the CURRENT THREAD ONLY and reverts the privilege when disposed. It supports nested calls on the same thread via a reference counter. WARNING: Because this relies on OS thread tokens, DO NOT use this across `await` points in async methods.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo
member.summary: Provides properties of Efi application files, such as architecture, application provider, and file information

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.FullName
member.summary: Full path to the executable file, including the partition path

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.Application
member.summary: The name of the folder in which the EFI executable is stored is the name of the provider of the executable file

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.FileInfo
member.summary: Executable's file information

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.Architecture
member.summary: Executable architecture based on Boot manager docs

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.#ctor(System.IO.FileInfo)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of from info of efi application file

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of from the full path to the efi application file

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.#ctor(Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of located on the ESP from the name of the application provider and its architecture with the default application name "boot"

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.#ctor(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of located on the ESP from the name of the application provider and application file name

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.#ctor(System.IO.DriveInfo,Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of located on external drive from the and its architecture with the default application name "boot"

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiExecutableInfo.#ctor(System.IO.DriveInfo,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new instance of located on external drive from the name of the application provider and application file name

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiPartition
member.summary: System partition containing boot files of operating systems

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiPartition.Identificator
member.summary: GUID identifier of the system EFI partition. This is a unique identifier assigned to the partition when it is created, and can be used to identify the partition among other partitions on the same disk. It is not related to the partition type, and is not a constant value. It can be used to access the partition's files and directories or passed to Win32 API functions that require a volume path.

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiPartition.TypeID
member.summary: GUID identifier of the system EFI partition type. This is a constant value defined by the UEFI specification for EFI System Partitions, and is used to identify partitions that are intended to be used as EFI System Partitions.

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.EfiPartition.VolumePath
member.summary: Full path to the system EFI partition, in the form of "\\?\Volume{GUID}\". This path can be used to access the partition's files and directories or passed to Win32 API functions that require a volume path. Note that this path is not a drive letter and cannot be used with functions that expect a drive letter.

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture
member.summary: Enumeration of supported processor architectures for firmware applications

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.Unknown
member.summary: Unknown architecture

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.Ia32
member.summary: 32-bit

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.x64
member.summary: AMD64

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.Ia64
member.summary: Intel Itanium 64

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.Arm
member.summary: AArch32 architecture

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.AArch64
member.summary: AArch64 architecture

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.RISC_V32
member.summary: RISC-V 32-bit architecture

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.RISC_V64
member.summary: RISC-V 64-bit architecture

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.SystemPartition.FirmwareApplicationArchitecture.RISC_V128
member.summary: RISC-V 128-bit architecture

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes
member.summary: Attributes bitmask to set for the variable

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.NON_VOLATILE
member.summary: The variable is accessible from a non volatile environment

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.BOOTSERVICE_ACCESS
member.summary: The variable is available while the Boot service is running

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.RUNTIME_ACCESS
member.summary: The variable is available at runtime

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.HARDWARE_ERROR_RECORD
member.summary: NoDescription

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.AUTHENTICATED_WRITE_ACCESS
member.summary: The variable is available only to authorized sources

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.TIME_BASED_AUTHENTICATED_WRITE_ACCESS
member.summary: NoDescription

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.APPEND_WRITE
member.summary: NoDescription

member.name: F:Unified.Firmware.VariableAttributes.ENHANCED_AUTHENTICATED_ACCESS
member.summary: NoDescription

member.name: T:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath
member.summary: Represents a strongly-typed volume path based on a unique volume identifier (GUID). Provides comparison, equality, and conversion operations for working with volume paths in a type-safe manner.
member.remarks: Use this struct to encapsulate and manipulate volume paths identified by GUIDs, enabling type-safe operations and conversions to and from related types such as Guid, string, and DirectoryInfo. Instances are immutable and can be compared or used in collections that require equality or ordering. Implicit conversions simplify interoperability with APIs that accept Guid or string representations of volume paths.
member.param: The unique identifier (GUID) that specifies the volume path associated with this instance.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.#ctor(System.Guid)
member.summary: Represents a strongly-typed volume path based on a unique volume identifier (GUID). Provides comparison, equality, and conversion operations for working with volume paths in a type-safe manner.
member.remarks: Use this struct to encapsulate and manipulate volume paths identified by GUIDs, enabling type-safe operations and conversions to and from related types such as Guid, string, and DirectoryInfo. Instances are immutable and can be compared or used in collections that require equality or ordering. Implicit conversions simplify interoperability with APIs that accept Guid or string representations of volume paths.
member.param: The unique identifier (GUID) that specifies the volume path associated with this instance.

member.name: P:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.Identificator
member.summary: Gets the unique identifier associated with this instance.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the VolumePath class using the specified string representation of a GUID.
member.param: A string containing the GUID that identifies the volume path. Must be a valid GUID format.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.ToString
member.summary: Returns the string representation of the volume in the Windows device path format.
member.remarks: This format is commonly used for referencing volumes in Windows APIs and system utilities. The returned string can be used for operations that require the full device path of a volume.
member.returns: A string containing the volume path in the format "\\?\Volume{GUID}\", where GUID is the value of the Identificator property.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.op_Implicit(Unified.Firmware.VolumePath)~System.Guid
member.summary: Converts a instance to its associated identifier.
member.param: The object to convert to a .

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.op_Implicit(System.Guid)~Unified.Firmware.VolumePath
member.summary: Defines an implicit conversion from a to a VolumePath instance.
member.param: The value representing the unique identifier of the volume path to convert.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.op_Implicit(Unified.Firmware.VolumePath)~System.String
member.summary: Converts a instance to its string representation.
member.param: The instance to convert.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.op_Implicit(System.String)~Unified.Firmware.VolumePath
member.summary: Converts a string containing a volume GUID path to a instance.
member.param: A string representing the volume GUID path to convert. Must not be null or empty.

member.name: M:Unified.Firmware.VolumePath.op_Implicit(Unified.Firmware.VolumePath)~System.IO.DirectoryInfo
member.summary: Converts a instance to a representing the same file system path.
member.param: The to convert to a .
